title: Soaring pump prices drive US inflation to highest level in almost two years
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Inflation reached its highest level in nearly two years, driven by soaring pump prices due to the U.S. war with Iran. Gasoline prices spiked significantly in Ma
excerpt: Inflation reached its highest level in nearly two years, driven by soaring pump prices due to the U.S. war with Iran. Gasoline prices spiked significantly in March, contributing to a nearly 1% inflation increase. The inflation rate peaked at 3.3% in recent months.

content.introduction: Inflation reached its highest level in nearly two years, driven by soaring pump prices due to the U.S. war with Iran. Gasoline prices spiked significantly in March, contributing to a nearly 1% inflation increase. The inflation rate peaked at 3.3% in recent months.
body: A surge in prices at the pump due to the Iran war has pushed the inflation rate to 3.3%.
From there, it rose sharply over the next two years, hitting 9.0% in the year to June 2022, before falling sharply back to 3.1% by June 2023.

Gasoline prices jumped more than a dollar a gallon in March, pushing inflation higher. Gasoline prices jumped more than a dollar a gallon in March, pushing inflation higher. The U.S. war with Iran and the resulting spike in energy prices have pushed inflation to its highest level in nearly two years. From February to March alone, prices rose nearly 1%, and the lion's share of that increase was at the gas pump as a result of the war and the shutdown of tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. You know, after years of complaining about high grocery prices, we actually saw a little decrease at the grocery store last month, but it didn't make much difference because of that wartime spike in gas prices. HORSLEY: One thing that's helping to cushion the blow of higher gas prices and prop up that spending is that a lot of people are getting tax refunds right now.
